title: Progressive taxation and tax brackets
description: Practice understanding of progressive taxation and tax brackets, comparing individuals with different incomes.

assessment_items.question_content:
A country has a progressive tax system and uses the following tax brackets:

Income| Tax rate 
- | -
$\$0 - \$19{,}999$ | $0\%$
$\$20{,}000 - \$29{,}999$ | $10\%$
$\$30{,}000+$ | $20\%$

You made $\$19{,}000$ while living in this country.

**How much do you owe in taxes?**

$\$$[[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints: Start by identifying which tax bracket this income falls in.

$\$19{,}000$ falls in the first tax bracket, since $\$19{,}000$ is between $\$0$ and $\$19{,}999$.

Income| Tax rate 
- | -
$\$0 - \$19{,}999$ | $0\%$
The tax rate for a $\$19{,}000$ income is $0\%$, which means you multiply $\$19{,}000 \times 0\%=\$0$.

assessment_items.question_content:
A country has a progressive tax system and uses the following tax brackets:

Income| Tax rate 
- | -
$\$0 - \$19{,}999$ | $0\%$
$\$20{,}000 - \$29{,}999$ | $10\%$
$\$30{,}000+$ | $20\%$

**Which of these incomes result in no taxes owed?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Martha makes $\$25{,}000$ and Edward makes $\$27{,}800$ a year at the same company doing different jobs. They both pay taxes based on the following tax brackets:

Income| Tax rate 
- | -
$\$0 - \$29{,}999$ | $0\%$
$\$30{,}000 - \$50{,}999$ | $10\%$
$\$51{,}000+$ | $20\%$

**Which statement is true?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Martha owes more in taxes.
Edward owes more in taxes.
Both Martha and Edward owe $\$0$ in taxes.
There is not enough information to decide who owes taxes.

assessment_items.question_content:
Income tax is an example of a progressive tax.

**Which of the following statements is true about progressive taxes?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The more you earn, the higher the tax rate.
The more you earn, the less you take home.
The same tax rate applies to all income earners.
Only the highest earners pay taxes.

assessment_items.question_content:
Income tax is an example of a progressive tax.

**Which of the following statements is true about progressive taxes?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Income is divided into tax brackets, and each bracket is taxed at a different rate.
The more you earn, the less you take home.
The same tax rate applies to all income earners.
Only the highest earners pay taxes.

assessment_items.question_content:
Income tax is an example of a progressive tax.

**Which of the following statements is true about progressive taxes?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The income of the highest earners gets divided across multiple tax brackets.
The more you earn, the less you take home.
The same tax rate applies to all income earners.
Only the highest earners pay taxes.

assessment_items.question_content:
The table below represents the yearly salaries of three individuals who live and work in the United States. The United States has a progressive income tax system. 

Teacher | Nurse | Store manager
:-: | :-: | :-:
$\$55{,}000$ | $\$72{,}000$  | $\$47{,}000$ 

**Which of these individuals will pay the most in taxes, based on salary alone?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: teacher
nurse
store manager
they will all pay the same

assessment_items.question_content:
The table below represents the yearly salaries of three individuals who live and work in the United States. The United States has a progressive income tax system. 

 Realtor | Truck driver | Office assistant
:-: | :-: | :-:
$\$92{,}000$  | $\$88{,}000$ |$\$55{,}000$ 

**Which of these individuals will pay the least in taxes, based on salary alone?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: office assistant
realtor
truck driver
they will all pay the same

assessment_items.question_content:
The table below represents the yearly salaries of three individuals who live and work in the United States. The United States has a progressive income tax system. 

Doctor | Accountant | Veterinarian
:-: | :-: | :-:
$\$170{,}000$ | $\$65{,}000$  | $\$77{,}000$ 

**Which of these individuals will pay the most in taxes, based on salary alone?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: accountant
doctor
veterinarian
they will all pay the same

assessment_items.question_content:
Your new job salary puts you in the $20\%$ tax bracket, the third one in the tax system.

**Which of the following best describes how much of your income you actually pay in taxes?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: You'll pay exactly $20\%$ of your income in taxes.
You'll pay more than $20\%$ of your income in taxes.
You'll pay less than $20\%$ of your whole income in taxes.
The amount of taxes you pay has no connection to your income.

assessment_items.question_content:
You've recently started a new job with a salary that puts you in the $22\%$ tax bracket. This means a part of your income is taxed at $22\%$.

**How is the rest of your income taxed?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: at a rate lower than $22\%$
at a rate higher than $22\%$
It is not taxed.

assessment_items.question_content:
You got a $\$2{,}000$ pay raise that increased your salary from $\$50{,}000$ to $\$52{,}000$. This pushed you into a $21\%$ tax bracket.

**Which amount will be taxed at $21\%$**?

[[☃ radio 1]]
